vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1370: Editor & file-navigation integration — wire up Open picker, port MarkdownFileLink chip menu, add file Quickview, expand editor registry

plan.title: Editor & file-navigation integration: wire up Open picker, port MarkdownFileLink chip menu, add file Quickview, expand editor registry
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: PAN-966 shipped the server launcher (open.ts, editor.ts, RPC wiring) and created PanOpenInPicker — but the picker is rendered nowhere (dead code) and ChatMarkdown.tsx:280 still renders every link as a naive external <a target="_blank">. There is no file-path detection, no chip styling, no right-click menu, no Quickview, no Kiro, no JetBrains family. T3Code is ~280 commits ahead of our last sync and has the polished UX we want to port.
narratives.Proposal: Five-phase delivery (15 beads): (A) expand contracts editor registry with Kiro + JetBrains family and port their icons; (B) wire PanOpenInPicker into CommandDeck OverviewTab + IssueDrawer with a default Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+O keybinding; (C) port the three T3Code utility modules verbatim (filePathDisplay, markdown-links, contextMenuFallback) — t3→pan renames only; (D) build MarkdownFileLink chip component with right-click Open/Copy-relative/Copy-full menu, then integrate into ChatMarkdown by adding a cwd prop and replacing the naive <a> renderer; (E) add a pan.readWorkspaceFile RPC with path-traversal validation and build the Shift+hover Quickview popover that consumes it. Full keybindings settings editor (T3 #2533), pinned-side-panel Quickview (Option B), and modal Quickview (Option C) are explicit follow-ups, not in this issue.

autoDecisions.summary: Render PanOpenInPicker in two surfaces: CommandDeck OverviewTab (next to existing workspace.data.path line at OverviewTab.tsx:912) and the IssueDrawer workspace section via WorkspaceStatusOverview.
autoDecisions.rationale: The #966 PRD called for 'split-button next to workspace path'. The codebase has no InspectorPanel.tsx (the issue body's phrasing); the equivalent surfaces are OverviewTab (which already renders workspace.data.path with a primitive vscode:// link to replace) and IssueDrawer (the user-facing inspector). Both consume workspace.path from WorkspaceInfo, so no new fetch is needed.

autoDecisions.summary: Quickview ships as Option A (Shift+hover popover with Shiki snippet).
autoDecisions.rationale: Cheapest implementation, matches the 'quick' intent in the feature name, leverages our existing Shiki dependency, and Shift+hover doesn't conflict with the existing click semantics (left-click = open in editor). Options B (pinned side panel) and C (modal) are explicitly noted as follow-ups in the issue.

autoDecisions.summary: Modifier key for Quickview is Shift, not Ctrl/Cmd or Alt.
autoDecisions.rationale: Ctrl/Cmd+click conventionally means 'open in background tab'; Alt/Option is often OS-claimed (e.g. macOS option-click). Shift is the least overloaded modifier.

autoDecisions.summary: Quickview trigger is hover, not click.
autoDecisions.rationale: Click already means 'open in editor' on a chip. Using hover for Quickview avoids any modifier-aware click-handler branching and matches the implicit 'preview' affordance — you don't commit to opening anything by hovering.

autoDecisions.summary: Path-traversal validation in pan.readWorkspaceFile uses path.resolve + prefix check + realpath re-check to defeat both '../' escapes and symlink escapes. Max file size 256 KiB.
autoDecisions.rationale: Standard defense for any RPC that exposes file reads scoped to a directory. 256 KiB is plenty for Quickview snippets; larger files truncate with a clear marker.

autoDecisions.summary: Keybinding for editor.openFavorite is Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+O, plumbed through a stub config map (single key) — NOT a full keybindings system.
autoDecisions.rationale: T3Code #2533's keybindings settings editor is a separate larger port the issue explicitly defers. MVP only needs one default binding so the picker can advertise it. The stub leaves a clean extension point for a future keybindings system.

autoDecisions.summary: Editor detection cache is left intentionally unchanged — restart required to pick up new editors.
autoDecisions.rationale: PanOpenLive resolves editors once at layer construction (open.ts:97). `which` calls are slow on cold filesystems. Adding cache invalidation for a workflow that needs a server restart anyway (new editor installed = new dashboard up) is over-engineering. Documented in hazards.

autoDecisions.summary: T3 → pan rename only; preserve T3 file and identifier names where practical.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue body explicitly requires this — keeps future upstream merges from T3Code cheap (drift tracker #703).

Panopticon has no desktop bridge yet; T3Code's `api.contextMenu.show` is Electron/Tauri-only. Shipping the web fallback alone is sufficient and ports easily. A future native bridge can drop in behind the existing shim.

autoDecisions.summary: Shiki for Quickview syntax highlighting (no new dependency).
autoDecisions.rationale: Already used by CodeBlock at ChatMarkdown.tsx:276 plus SpecialistLogViewer, PlanDAG, SystemHealthPill, etc. Same theme/language registry, no bundle-size cost.

items.title: Expand EDITORS registry in @panctl/contracts to include Kiro + full JetBrains family

narrative.Action: In packages/contracts/src/editor.ts, append entries for Kiro plus IntelliJ IDEA, PyCharm, WebStorm, GoLand, CLion, Rider, RubyMine, RustRover, DataGrip, DataSpell, Aqua, PhpStorm. Mirror the structure of T3Code origin/main's OpenInPicker.tsx resolveOptions list (command names + supportsGoto). Extend the EditorIdSchema union accordingly. Rebuild contracts so the type literal is available to consumers.

subItems.title: EDITORS array contains 13 new entries (Kiro + 12 JetBrains) with correct ids, labels, commands, and supportsGoto flags matching T3Code origin/main verbatim
EditorIdSchema literal union includes every new id; TypeScript compiles across server + frontend
packages/contracts builds cleanly (npm run build inside packages/contracts) and the workspace root typecheck passes

items.title: Port JetBrains + Kiro icons to the frontend

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/EditorIcons.tsx exporting a JetBrainsIcons map and a Kiro icon. Port the SVG sources from T3Code origin/main's OpenInPicker.tsx JetBrainsIcons (and Kiro icon). PanOpenInPicker should resolve editor → icon via the existing EDITORS list; add a small helper `editorIconFor(id)` to centralize the lookup.

subItems.title: Every editor in the EDITORS registry has a renderable icon component (no missing-icon fallback in the picker dropdown)
Icons are SVG components with the same visual style as T3Code upstream (no raster bitmaps)

items.title: Add server-side tests covering Kiro + JetBrains detection

narrative.Action: Extend src/dashboard/server/services/__tests__/open.test.ts with cases mocking `which`/`where` for the new editor commands. Verify resolveAvailableEditors returns the expected EditorId set when each is installed/absent. No production-code change required — detection is data-driven by the EDITORS list.

subItems.title: Test passes when only Kiro is installed → result contains 'kiro' and no JetBrains ids
Test passes when full JetBrains family is installed → result contains every JetBrains id

items.title: Render PanOpenInPicker in CommandDeck OverviewTab next to the workspace path

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/ZoneCOverviewTabs/OverviewTab.tsx around line 912 (where `workspace.data.path` already renders), add `<PanOpenInPicker cwd={workspace.data.path} />`. Replace the primitive `vscode://file/${workspace.data.path}` link at line 941 with the picker (or keep both side-by-side initially, removing the primitive in a follow-up commit within the same bead).

subItems.title: Picker is visible next to the workspace path in OverviewTab; primary button label reflects the user's last-chosen editor (or first available if none)
Clicking the chevron lists every detected editor with its icon; selecting one calls pan.shellOpenInEditor and persists the choice via setPreferredEditor
Primitive `vscode://file/...` link at OverviewTab.tsx:941 is removed (superseded by the picker)

items.title: Render PanOpenInPicker in IssueDrawer workspace section

narrative.Action: Add the picker to src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/drawer/IssueDrawer.tsx where the workspace block renders (use WorkspaceStatusOverview as the natural mount point if it already shows workspace info in the drawer; otherwise add directly to IssueDrawer). Pull cwd from the workspace info fetched at IssueDrawer.tsx — workspace.path from `/api/workspaces/:issueId`.

subItems.title: Opening an issue drawer with an existing workspace shows the picker; cwd matches WorkspaceInfo.path
Selecting an editor in the drawer is immediately reflected in OverviewTab on next render (shared localStorage preference)
When no workspace exists for the issue, the picker is not rendered (no crash, no empty button)

items.title: Add Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+O keybinding that triggers editor.openFavorite

narrative.Action: Create a minimal stub at src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/keybindings.ts exposing a single `EDITOR_OPEN_FAVORITE_KEY = 'mod+shift+o'` constant and a `useEditorOpenFavoriteShortcut(cwd: string)` hook that listens on the dashboard root element (not window) for the binding, calls `pan.shellOpenInEditor` with the preferred editor, and `preventDefault()`s the event. Wire the hook in OverviewTab and IssueDrawer so it's active whenever a workspace surface is mounted. Add tooltip text on the picker button advertising the shortcut.

subItems.title: Pressing Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+O while a workspace surface is mounted opens the preferred editor (same call path as clicking the primary picker button)
The shortcut does not fire on surfaces with no workspace context (no NPE, no spurious opens)
Picker primary button shows the binding in its tooltip (e.g. 'Open in Cursor (⌘⇧O)')

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/filePathDisplay.ts. Copy `formatWorkspaceRelativePath` verbatim from T3Code origin/main's apps/web/src/filePathDisplay.ts (t3→pan rename only). The function strips the workspace root from an absolute path while keeping the workspace root folder's basename as a prefix (NOT Node `path.relative` semantics). Export the function and any helper types.

subItems.title: Module exports `formatWorkspaceRelativePath(absPath: string, workspaceRoot: string): string` with the exact T3Code semantics (basename of workspaceRoot retained as prefix)
Unit tests cover: path inside workspace, path equal to workspace root, path outside workspace (returns original or null per T3 semantics), trailing slashes

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/markdown-links.ts. Port `resolveMarkdownFileLinkMeta` verbatim from T3Code origin/main's apps/web/src/markdown-links.ts (t3→pan rename only). The function takes (href, cwd) and returns a `{ targetPath, displayPath, filePath } | null` triple: targetPath = absolute path + ':line:col' (used by Open-in-editor and Copy-full-path), displayPath = workspace-relative path (used as chip label and Copy-relative-path), filePath = absolute path without `:line:col` (used for the file-type icon resolver). Returns null when href is not a file-link candidate.

subItems.title: Module exports `resolveMarkdownFileLinkMeta(href: string, cwd: string): { targetPath, displayPath, filePath } | null` with T3Code's exact triple semantics

Unit tests cover: absolute path + ':L:C', absolute path + ':L', absolute path bare, workspace-relative path, relative-with-../, non-file href (https://, mailto:, #anchor — all return null), Windows path separators if T3 upstream supports them
When cwd is empty/undefined, the function returns null (does not produce a useless absolute=displayPath chip)

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/contextMenuFallback.ts. Port T3Code's web-fallback `contextMenu.show(items, position)` implementation (renders an absolutely-positioned menu div, dismisses on outside-click and Escape, supports per-item label + onClick). Do NOT port the native-bridge code path — that path is desktop-only and Panopticon has no bridge. Export a single `showContextMenu({ items, x, y })` function.

subItems.title: Module exports `showContextMenu` with the same item shape as T3Code's upstream contextMenu interface (label + onClick at minimum)
Menu dismisses on outside-click, Escape key, and after an item is selected
Menu auto-positions to stay inside the viewport when invoked near right/bottom edges

items.title: Build MarkdownFileLink chip component (rendering + hover tooltip + file-type icon)

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/MarkdownFileLink.tsx. Port T3Code's MarkdownFileLink (from apps/web/src/components/ChatMarkdown.tsx in origin/main). Renders a chip with file-type icon + displayPath label + optional ':L' suffix; hover tooltip shows targetPath. Left-click calls pan.shellOpenInEditor with the preferred editor (the integration with the actual ChatMarkdown renderer comes in `chatmarkdown-integration`).

subItems.title: Component renders a chip styled like T3Code upstream (file-type icon, monospace path label, `· L<n>` suffix when present)
Left-click opens the targetPath in the preferred editor (no menu)
Hover shows a tooltip with the absolute targetPath (so users can verify what they're about to open)
File-type icon comes from a small extension→icon helper (port T3's resolver where present, otherwise use a lucide File icon as fallback)

items.title: Wire right-click context menu on file chip: Open / Copy relative / Copy full

narrative.Action: In MarkdownFileLink.tsx, attach an onContextMenu handler that calls `showContextMenu(...)` from contextMenuFallback with three items: 'Open in editor' (calls shellOpenInEditor with targetPath), 'Copy relative path' (writes displayPath to clipboard), 'Copy full path' (writes targetPath to clipboard). Each emits a sonner toast on success/error. Preserve T3Code's item ordering and labels verbatim.

subItems.title: Right-clicking a file chip shows exactly three items in the order Open in editor / Copy relative path / Copy full path
Copy relative path writes displayPath (workspace-relative) to the clipboard; Copy full path writes targetPath (absolute with :L:C if present)
Each successful action emits a sonner toast; clipboard failures emit an error toast
Browser's native context menu does not also appear (preventDefault on onContextMenu)

items.title: Add cwd prop to ChatMarkdown, swap naive <a> for MarkdownFileLink, thread cwd to every callsite

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/ChatMarkdown.tsx: (1) add optional `cwd?: string` prop, threaded into makeComponents via closure; (2) in the `a()` renderer (line 280), call resolveMarkdownFileLinkMeta(href, cwd); when it returns a non-null triple, render <MarkdownFileLink meta={...} />, otherwise keep the existing safe-external-link renderer. (3) Then thread `cwd` to every ChatMarkdown callsite — MessagesTimeline.tsx (8 occurrences: lines 414, 547, 585, 610, 645, 828, 832 per grep) — by reading from the parent conversation's `cwd`. Callsites where cwd is genuinely unavailable (e.g. system-summary surfaces) pass `undefined` explicitly so the type system distinguishes 'forgot to thread' from 'intentionally omitted'.

subItems.title: ChatMarkdown accepts optional cwd?: string prop; type-safe (undefined disables file-chip rendering)
Assistant messages containing file paths (e.g. `package.json:1` or `/abs/path/to/file.ts:42:5`) render as MarkdownFileLink chips when cwd is provided
Non-file links (https://, mailto:, anchors) still render as safe external <a target=_blank> with the existing javascript:/data: blocklist intact
All ChatMarkdown callsites in MessagesTimeline.tsx (8 sites) and any other consumer pass cwd from the parent Conversation.cwd; sites without a cwd source explicitly pass undefined
Existing ChatMarkdown unit/integration tests still pass; new tests cover the file-link branch

items.title: Add pan.readWorkspaceFile RPC with path-traversal validation and size cap

narrative.Action: Add a new RPC in packages/contracts/src/rpc.ts: `pan.readWorkspaceFile` with payload `{ issueId: string, relativePath: string, line?: number, contextLines?: number }` and success `{ text: string, lang: string, truncated: boolean, totalLines: number }`. Implement server side as a new service or directly in ws-rpc.ts: resolve workspace path via `getWorkspacePathForIssue`, then `path.resolve(workspaceRoot, relativePath)` and assert the resolved path starts with `workspaceRoot + sep` (or equals it). After fs.readFile, also `fs.realpath` the resolved path and re-assert containment to defeat symlink escapes. Reject with PanRpcError code `PATH_OUTSIDE_WORKSPACE` on any escape. Cap file size at 256 KiB (read fs.stat first, return `truncated: true` if exceeded — read first 256 KiB and mark). Derive `lang` from file extension via a small map (ts, tsx, js, jsx, json, md, py, go, rs, etc.). Use fs/promises ONLY (no execSync per repo rule).

subItems.title: RPC schema added to packages/contracts and registered in ws-rpc.ts; type-safe end-to-end
Path-traversal attempts (relativePath starting with `../`, absolute paths outside workspace, symlinks pointing outside) are rejected with PanRpcError code PATH_OUTSIDE_WORKSPACE; covered by tests
Files larger than 256 KiB return `truncated: true` with the first 256 KiB; smaller files return `truncated: false`
`lang` field is set from file extension via a documented map; unknown extensions return 'plaintext'
Server implementation uses fs/promises only (no execSync, no readFileSync) per repo rules

items.title: Build Quickview Shift+hover popover with Shiki-highlighted snippet

narrative.Action: In MarkdownFileLink.tsx, attach onMouseEnter / onMouseLeave handlers gated on `event.shiftKey` (or a Shift-held state via window keydown/keyup listeners scoped to the chip). When Shift+hover begins, call pan.readWorkspaceFile with the chip's relativePath (derive from displayPath), wait for the result, then render a popover anchored to the chip showing a Shiki-highlighted snippet auto-scrolled to `:line` if present. Reuse src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/usePickerPosition.ts for viewport-aware positioning. Dismiss popover on mouse-out or Shift-release. Show a loading spinner if the read takes >100ms; show an error toast if the read fails (path-traversal rejection is a system error, surface it).

subItems.title: Shift+hover on a file chip shows a popover with a Shiki-highlighted snippet of the file, auto-scrolled to the chip's :line (or top of file if no line specified)
Popover dismisses on mouse-out and Shift-release without leaving stale popovers
Popover positions itself inside the viewport — chips near right/bottom edges do not cause clipping (uses usePickerPosition logic)
Plain left-click on the chip still opens in editor (Quickview hover does not break the click path)
Truncated files show a 'truncated — first 256 KiB' indicator in the popover footer

items.title: End-to-end tests for readWorkspaceFile RPC (traversal, success, truncation, lang)

narrative.Action: Add tests under src/dashboard/server/services/__tests__/ (or co-located with ws-rpc.ts) covering: (1) successful read of a small file inside the workspace, (2) ../ escape rejected, (3) absolute path outside workspace rejected, (4) symlink to /etc/passwd rejected (create a temp symlink in a test fixture and assert PanRpcError), (5) 1 MiB file returns truncated:true with first 256 KiB, (6) .ts file returns lang='typescript', .md returns lang='markdown', .xyz returns lang='plaintext'.

subItems.title: All 6 test scenarios above pass; tests are deterministic (use vi fake timers if any timing is involved per repo rule)
Tests use fs/promises and create their fixtures in a temp dir that is cleaned up in afterEach
